domain: Kon-Tiki expedition (1947 Heyerdahl deliberately unsteerable balsa raft; 101 days Pacific drift; landed Tuamotu islands via East-West currents; zero active steering; one-direction-only navigation)
specific_mechanism: Maximally passive transport: zero active steering; entirely environment-driven; broad-basin landing; one-direction only.
llm_application: KONTIKI-DRIFT: deploy LLM agents with NO EXPLICIT GOAL OR REWARD — only an initial seed prompt; rely on the agent's training-distribution prior (its 'prevailing wind') to drift toward broad answer-regions; no tool-use, no retrieval, no replanning, no reward shaping. Useful as a BASELINE measurement of training-prior-alignment direction: where does the model's default behavior 'drift' when not actively pulled? Different from goal-conditioned agents and from 'what LLMs do when left alone' (which still has some steering) — explicit ZERO-STEERING + BROAD-BASIN CONVERGENCE-MEASUREMENT protocol.
novelty_claim: Goal-conditioned RL agents have explicit reward. ReAct/Reflexion/CRITIC have explicit observation+update. 'What LLMs do when left alone' (2509.21224) lets agents explore but with default tools. KONTIKI-DRIFT differs by TOTAL ZERO-STEERING + ONLY initial seed + measure broad-basin convergence direction — explicitly a measurement protocol for the model's default drift direction.
